_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Kampung Baru Master Redevelopment Still Stalled After 40 Years
context: The Kampung Baru master redevelopment plan (a 121-hectare project costed at around RM43 billion) remains stalled. PKB's separate Kampung Sungai Baru sub-project (328 owners) finally moved forward in 2025 after years of dispute, but the larger Kampung Baru proper has not begun.

cards.text: 40 years, zero ground broken on main Kampung Baru, even as Kampung Sungai Baru sub-project finally moved forward in 2025.
cards.sub: PKB's master plan envisages ~RM43 billion across 121 hectares with 17,500 residential units. Fragmented Malay Reserve Land ownership slows consolidation.

cards.h: 121 hectares of Kampung Baru sit beside the KLCC core: economically prime but legally complicated by Malay Reserve Land status.
cards.s: Land in Kampung Baru is some of the most valuable in KL on a per-square-foot basis, yet the master plan has not produced a single completed redevelopment block.
cards.d: 121 hectares of Kampung Baru sit beside the KLCC core: economically prime but legally complicated by Malay Reserve Land status. Land in Kampung Baru is some of the most valuable in KL on a per-square-foot basis, yet the master plan has not produced a single completed redevelopment block.

cards.h: Thousands of Malay families have lived in Kampung Baru for generations. Any redevelopment means displacing a historic community.
cards.s: Cultural heritage value is real and is the central reason consolidation has been politically difficult across multiple administrations.
cards.d: Thousands of Malay families have lived in Kampung Baru for generations. Any redevelopment means displacing a historic community. Cultural heritage value is real and is the central reason consolidation has been politically difficult across multiple administrations.

cards.h: PKB (Perbadanan Pembangunan Kampong Bharu), set up in 2012, has not delivered the master redevelopment despite years of operational spending.
cards.s: Malay Reserve Land status creates legal complexity requiring individual consent from a large number of fragmented lot owners.
cards.d: PKB (Perbadanan Pembangunan Kampong Bharu), set up in 2012, has not delivered the master redevelopment despite years of operational spending. Malay Reserve Land status creates legal complexity requiring individual consent from a large number of fragmented lot owners.

cards.text: Kampung Baru is not a real estate problem. It is the unresolved question of whether Malay heritage and KLCC-grade land economics can coexist in the same blocks.
Like a historic building where the heirs cannot agree on renovation: 40 years of blueprints but the walls keep crumbling.
cards.sub: Fragmented ownership and competing visions have paralysed redevelopment for four decades. The Kg Sungai Baru breakthrough shows it can move when terms are right.

cards.text: The path forward is not a bigger plan. It is structured smaller-block consolidation like Kg Sungai Baru, with cultural heritage protections built into each phase.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
